# servicenow_business_rule (Data Source)

`servicenow_business_rule` data source can be used to retrieve information of a single business rule in ServiceNow by name.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Look up an existing business rule in ServiceNow by name.
data "servicenow_business_rule" "example" {
  name = "Example Business Rule"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Display name of the business rule.

### Read-Only

- `abort_action` (Boolean) If true, aborts the current action when the rule's condition is met.
- `action_delete` (Boolean) If true, the business rule fires on delete operations.
- `action_insert` (Boolean) If true, the business rule fires on insert operations.
- `action_query` (Boolean) If true, the business rule fires on query operations.
- `action_update` (Boolean) If true, the business rule fires on update operations.
- `active` (Boolean) Whether or not this business rule is enabled.
- `add_message` (Boolean) If true, an informational message is shown when the rule fires.
- `advanced` (Boolean) If true, the rule uses advanced (script-based) configuration. ServiceNow auto-sets this to true when a script is supplied.
- `client_callable` (Boolean) If true, this business rule can be called from client-side scripts.
- `condition` (String) The condition under which this business rule executes.
- `description` (String) Description of what this business rule does.
- `filter_condition` (String) An encoded query filter to determine when this business rule applies.
- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `is_rest` (Boolean) If true, this business rule applies to REST API operations.
- `order` (Number) The order in which this business rule executes relative to others on the same table.
- `priority` (Number) The priority of this business rule.
- `protection_policy` (String) Determines how application files are protected when downloaded or installed. Can be empty for no protection, 'read' for read-only protection or 'protected'.
- `role_conditions` (String) Comma-separated list of roles required to trigger this business rule.
- `scope` (String) Associates a resource to a specific application ID in ServiceNow.
- `script` (String) The JavaScript to execute when the business rule fires.
- `table` (String) Name of the table this business rule fires on. Cannot be changed once created.
- `when` (String) When the business rule should execute. Valid values are 'before', 'after', 'async', or 'display'.
